title: rubus caesius subsp. arvalis
intro: Rubus caesius subsp. arvalis, also known as the wild black raspberry or arctic bramble, is a subspecies of the black raspberry plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to its high concentration of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rubus caesius subsp. arvalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make jams, jellies, and teas, as well as dried and powdered extracts for medicinal purposes.
